reviews.review_title: Games gone, and I can’t get them back
reviews.review_description: I purchased all of the extra tables for this app, and they used to work on my old oculus. I bought an oculus three, and the store says I’ve purchased the extra tables, but the app itself says I don’t own them, and if I click on the buy button just to see what happens, they don’t come up anyway.  ￼

developer_response.body: Hi there! Sorry to hear that. We have seen similar reports about this problem, unfortunately. We spoke with Meta, and their team identified a platform-side issue that was affecting purchase recognition under certain conditions. Unfortunately, this also impacted Pinball FX Classic VR. They've informed us that a fix is currently rolling out through Quest firmware updates, so hopefully, you should be able to access your purchased tables soon. Thank you for the feedback!

reviews.review_description: The game itself is quite good but I bought the Jaws pack DLC and it doesn’t install. You can’t even get a refund because the base game has been played longer than 2 hours. Basically a $25 loss.  I remove this review once this issue is patched or a refund is arranged.

developer_response.body: Hi, sorry to hear about this! We talked to Meta, and their team identified a platform-side issue that was affecting purchase recognition, which impacted Pinball FX Classic VR. They've let us know that a fix is currently rolling out through Quest firmware updates. Hopefully, you should have access to your purchased tables soon. Thanks for your comments, we are happy to hear you otherwise enjoy the game.

reviews.review_description:
I really wish they’d migrate these tables to the VRFX2 app. It’s a pain in the butt going back and forth between games, especially since this one only has a few tables to offer. Why not combine the games?  I’ll change to 5 stars soon as that’s done
While you’re at it, combine the Star Wars tables as well. Seems very silly to have 3 games when it could all be included in 1.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Pinball FX Classic VR was developed in our own, older engine. Since we changed to a new game engine a few years ago, it was not possible to add new tables to our previous title. Furthermore, we wanted to give a fresh start to our new VR pinball adventure. 
As Pinball FX VR features tables that were not released in VR before, we don't have information regarding transfers from other VR titles, but it would be nice to be able to make that happen in the future.

reviews.review_description: Between Oculus and Zen Studios what a couple of dirty companies they could not tell people open a steam account to access the content but of course they don't want to tell you you need a gaming computer to do it what untrustworthy companies

developer_response.body: Hi, sorry to hear you lost access to your purchased tables! We talked to Meta, and their team identified a platform-side issue that was affecting purchase recognition, which impacted Pinball FX Classic VR. They've let us know that a fix is currently rolling out through Quest firmware updates. Hopefully, you should have access to your purchased tables soon. Thanks for your comments.

reviews.review_description: I have loved Zen pinball since the first Xbox version. Probably spending several hundred dollars on even the same tables over various platforms. Now my Oculus tables are missing, I'm not buying Jaws for the fifth time. Too bad because I really love everything Zen does for the pinball lovers but this has to be fixed please.

developer_response.body: Hi, sorry to hear that! There is currently a problem with accessing table purchases, unfortunately. We talked to Meta, and their team identified a platform-side issue that was affecting purchase recognition and impacting the game. They've let us know that a fix is currently rolling out through Quest firmware updates. Hopefully, you should regain access to your tables soon. Thank you for your feedback and for your continuous support over the years.

reviews.review_description: Since updating to the latest version of Meta Horizon OS, Pinball FX Classic VR DLC is unplayable for the Q3!? But playable for Q2! Also the achievements frequently will pop up, when I’ve already gotten them!? The Q2 is more stable than the Q3, when it should be the other way around!

developer_response.body: Hi! Could you please elaborate on the issue you faced in the game? Thank you in advance!

reviews.review_description: Can't access ANY of the DLC I paid for.....ony can access 3 tables...Mars, some western one and an aquatic one.....no Universal or Walking dead tables. I cant even get a refund.......please patch.

developer_response.body: Hi, we are sorry to hear that! We spoke with Meta, and their team identified a platform-side issue that was affecting purchase recognition under certain conditions. Unfortunately, this also impacted Pinball FX Classic VR. They've informed us that a fix is currently rolling out through Quest firmware updates, so hopefully, you should be able to access your purchased tables soon. Thank you for your honest review!

reviews.review_description: I love all the games on here.  A couple months ago, I lost all my achievements and the game no longer shows leaderboard info.    Is this a terrible update or is there a glitch?

developer_response.body: Hi, sorry to hear that! I'll forward your report to the developer team, so they can look for possible solutions. Thank you for your review.

reviews.review_description: Bought the dlc bundle for $32, the dlc has not become available after more than 24 hours. Refund refused because iv played the base game for more than 2 hours, the dlc cannot be independently refunded, ill have to go through my cc now.

developer_response.body: Hi, sorry to hear about this! We talked to Meta, and their team identified a platform-side issue that was affecting purchase recognition, which impacted Pinball FX Classic VR. They've let us know that a fix is currently rolling out through Quest firmware updates. Hopefully, you should have access to your purchased tables soon. In the meantime, could you please restart your headset and make sure it is updated to the latest available firmware version? Thanks for your comments.

reviews.review_description: Only gave it a one star due to buying the dlc and it's not letting you play it in game. I can't get a refund so lost out till it gets sorted. I should have read the reviews before buying as it seems a common issue with this game.

developer_response.body: Hi there! Yes, unfortunately, we have seen similar reports from other players. We spoke with the Meta team, and they identified a platform-side issue that was affecting purchase recognition under certain conditions. This also impacted Pinball FX Classic VR. They've informed us that a fix is currently rolling out through Quest firmware updates, so hopefully, you should be able to access your purchased tables soon. Thank you for your honest review!

reviews.review_description: I love this game. I had a lots of points, because I try to delete that. It successed, but I can't find my extra DLS tables. How  I can reinstall them?

developer_response.body: Hi! There is currently a problem with accessing table purchases, unfortunately. We talked to Meta, and their team identified a platform-side issue that was affecting purchase recognition and impacting the game. They've let us know that a fix is currently rolling out through Quest firmware updates. Hopefully, you should regain access to your tables soon. Thanks for your comments.

reviews.review_description: I purchased all the additional tables. Today the additional tables are missing. Deleted the app and still missing meta was useless

developer_response.body: Hi Chuckletterpress, we are sorry to hear that! We spoke with Meta, and their team identified a platform-side issue that was affecting purchase recognition under certain conditions. Unfortunately, this also impacted Pinball FX Classic VR. They've informed us that a fix is currently rolling out through Quest firmware updates, so hopefully, you should be able to access your purchased tables soon. Thank you for your honest review!

reviews.review_description: Paid for all DLC now gone.  just 3 original tables. I want what i paid for.

developer_response.body: Hi there! We have seen similar reports from other players too, unfortunately. We spoke with Meta, and their team identified a platform-side issue that was affecting purchase recognition under certain conditions. Unfortunately, this also impacted Pinball FX Classic VR. They've informed us that a fix is currently rolling out through Quest firmware updates, so hopefully, you should be able to access your purchased tables soon. Thank you for your honest review!

reviews.review_description: Just installed the haptics kit for the xarcade2tv-xr and it includes the new dongle that works with this and Star Wars Pinball VR. It's GLORIOUS! You can also buy the dongle separately but it's nice to feel the ball rolling around the playfield. Make sure you adjust the sound settings - otherwise it won't sound that great. [remove spaces in the following link] h tt p://bit. ly/46exnab
I love pinball. This game brings me back to the good old days of the pinball arcade!
This needs to merge with pinball vr fx, or have x arcade compatability added.
Devs, please migrate these tables to pinball fx vr in their separate “classics” wing of the arcade. Thanks!

I have to say these are the most awesome pinball games I've ever played you guys do an amazing job I mean freaking amazing the graphics on these and the love that you put into it with the background and animations and all that are just freaking amazing I am blown away I love love love these pinball machines please please please make more may I suggest like the Titanic Halloween themed ones more movie related ones just keep them coming please please love love them if you read this and you love pinball you  must buy these no matter what they are freaking amazing good job guys please keep them coming don't stop love your pinball machines highly recommended AAA+++
Minus one star for the low resolution. You have to rely on QGO to get a sharp image. Unfortunately, the developer hasn’t updated the app for Quest 3.

Developers stripped away games in favor of DLCs and renamed the game "Classic" AFTER it was already purchased. Apparently they feel they can do whatever they want after they have your money. 

They also came out with a new version called Pinball FX VR heavily favoring expensive DLCs. Honestly, I don't mind adding more tables to my game's library through DLCs, but not at their cost, and certainly not for a bait and switch developer. I wouldn't trust these guys at Zen Studios at all. Boo! 👎

Edit: its now downloading tables,but this just worries me, that one day they can just pull the plug and you wont be able to get the items you paid for. I do not understand why the tables dont download packed with the game. 


Just like many others, i have bought this on sale and all the DLC. Only to be told ingame that i can not download the DLC with the error ''an error has occurred while downloading''. If i try to boot the game while it is downloading i get a different error ''com.zenstudios.pinballfx2vrquest uses an online service that no longer works with this device. Some features may not behave as intended.''
So i have paid £20 for a game that does not work as intended, no way of reaching Zen as their website doesn't work correctly, their discord wont let me in to tell them, i have also tried emailing directly. Meta won't assist me with this issue saying it is a Zen problem and to reach out to them. Refunding the DLC is not easy either you will have to talk to Meta directly. I would hold off buying this game right now even though it is on offer just on the fact that it doesn't work as intended and a few other people have had the same issue so it is not just a ''me'' problem. Shame really

developer_response.body: Hi wolfychaos, thank you for your feedback! We were experiencing server interruptions, this might be the reason that you had trouble accessing your DLCs. Our goal is not to pull away any purchases that have been made by our players, but an issue like this comes unexpected and it may affect players profiles and their connection. I hope we can avoid issues like this happening again!

reviews.review_description:
High quality pinball game. Pinball FX VR is better though. Similar visual quality between the two.

The environmental graphics are pretty involved in this one. I’m a fan of them here.

I enjoyed the Jaws, secrets of the Deep, Walking Dead and Paranormal tables.

I just bought the base game and all the dlc for a total of 20 quid - 12 tables! They downloaded fine and I recorded a video of all the tables for youtube and reddit. Pinball VR FX is Zens latest pinball arcade title, but this one still holds up and the tables are different. The experience is very streamlined, you only see 3 tables, you can't move, you can cycle the tables when you look at them and press RT and LT - but you don't have hands. It loads super fast though!
I bought both The Walking Dead and Universal tables, and none of the tables will download. Hopefully, this issue will be taken care of in a few days, or I will have to ask for a refund.

developer_response.body: Hi Ca_Folfer, thank you for your feedback! Sorry to hear about your experiences. We were experiences with our servers at that time but now it seems that everything is working as it should. May I ask if you were able to access your purchases? Thanks in advance!

reviews.review_description: Just paid for the Universal tables as an add-on but getting error messages when I try to download them. Can't find a way to message anyone to sort this issue.

developer_response.body: Hi kymjets, thank you for your review! Sorry to hear about your experiences, we were experiences server interruptions at that time, may I ask if your connection has improved since then? If this is not the case, please make sure to contact us at support@zenstudios.com!

reviews.review_description: I really loved playing this but 2 of the 3 games disappeared, and now there is only one game for the price and the additional games are $10 a piece. Why were the 2 removed? It made the price of the game worth it, but now I’m just disappointed.

I’ve always loved how each table has its own surrounding environment.
These are some of Zen’s best original tables.
But what stands out the most, having played other versions, is that the anti-aliasing is really improved in this version.
It’s so smooth with almost no jaggies.
I play Pinball FX VR mixed reality, but this is still good enough to make time for.
With all that said, I would still love it if the mixed reality option was added here.
But, you should still be able to look at your local
high scores. Not quite right without that.

reviews.review_title: Great way to satisfy that pinball fix
reviews.review_description: The realism is uncanny as the ball behaves as it would in a real machine, with the sounds, lights ...it's just a great experience.  I've experienced no problems with any of the tables so I'm a little confused with some of the other comments. There is a slight lag when selecting which tables you want in the three spots though, but that's about it.   Also in response to one of the earlier comments, you can actually  adjust the table height and distance  from you  in the settings so you can get a pretty close view of  the action without having to bend down, I found it more enjoyable this way.

I tried to love it, but it's very glitchy. The pinball flippers will get stuck up on a regular basis and make you lose the ball, especially if you got the joysticks, that shake the machine, even by accident.   It's annoying enough, I wish I had requested a refund.
Don't waste your money, the flippers constantly stick, making this game really unplayable
Please make it so I can pull up on the table to make it so I don’t have to keep my head down to see the game. My neck gets sore after a couple minutes.

Always played pinball back in my younger years but this takes it to another level! I soooo wish this was interactive so you can play an opponent competitively on the same machine. Also I hope to see more machines in the future.

reviews.review_title: ZEN is the Master of Time Travel!!!
reviews.review_description: Like the good ole days of going to your favorite arcade hangout and pumping quarter after quarter to master the silver ball ZEN has recreated that feeling and so much more. The effects that VR can pull off makes the pinball playing experience so much more involved. If you get the base game do yourself a favor and get the Universal  game pack all three tables are outstanding but for me the Back to the Future is jaw dropping. Having owned a real BTTF pinball I really got goosebumps playing this machine as the effects are insane and the gameplay is hands down the best VR pinball I have ever played. Travel back with Marty and Doc to all the great parts of the BTTF trilogy and play the future of pinball at its finest!!!
If you're spending money on tables etc... Just get it on PCVR. If that's an option for you. It's way better! But this version works okay

reviews.review_title: Very good but needs more tables!
reviews.review_description: This game is made very well, so good in fact that it's a real shame that the Devs don't seem to be supporting it anymore. I bought it as it was on sale and not being a big pinball player before, I can't stop playing this and ended up purchasing the universal tables. I just wish there was a Jurassic Park DLC.

I’m older and played a lot of pinball when I was younger.  It is way better than in real life.  The way the tables can transform and the people and object that float around d are very    Highly recommend this game, you will enjoy it.
I am a big fan of their tablet pinball. This is so much better. Love it. Hope they keep adding games as I will buy them.

reviews.review_title: Almost perfect - good 4ppl w motion sick
reviews.review_description:
A good interpretation of a classic pinball machine. There is some warping on the thinest ball rails. With Quest 3, turn up hz to 120 and even the most motion sick can play as they are basically static and follow the ball with their eyes.

The three machines that come with the basic purchase leave some to desire in intuitively as where to hit for specific missions in game. 

Suggest turning of background graphics and turn down music a bit for better immersion.

Overall a very nice to have game. Simple enough to get anyone on board with VR, be it 5 or 95 year old. 

Would like to give it 4.6 rating.
I guess Quest 3 version could handle a graphic update to be equal or close to PC version.
This is an awesome pinball game it feels so real and the challengers make it a game worth playing, I've spent hours playing it and highly recommend it, if you like pinball you'll love epic quests.

For $20 you only get three tables. The mars one is terrible, the nautical one is confusing, and the castle one is also confusing. There's no tutorials on what you're actually supposed to aim for. I'm a huge pinball fan and have it on my PS5 from this same company, but this is a half baked expensive product. Disappointed. Also the $$ add on tables packs don't even tell you what tables you're buying!

It only comes with 3 tables, the rest you have to buy. Honestly thought there would be more variety. Those 3 included tables are nice to play in bed if I can't sleep, but otherwise it gets boring real quick. Also, you can't rebind the table bump / tilt left joystick press to stop the ball from going right down the centre, which makes it difficult to use in a split second when you need it. The new Racket Pinball game is more fun if they add extra tables.
But you abandoned it, so you get one star! All kinds of tables came out for every other system and console except the Quest and SteamVR. Sad.

This was the first VR game that I got into probably back in 2016 or 2017 and I still enjoy it today. The environments are so fun, each matches the theme of the pinball game you are currently playing and they are very immersive. 

I’ve heard tell that some folks say this game is expensive but I think it averages out to about $5 per pinball machine and that is super reasonable! In real life, I easily go through $5 in quarters playing pinball and I don’t then own that machine 🤣

I have played this game for many hours and it’s nostalgic for me, as it was my first VR game that I was passionate about. I do play sitting, though, so it isn’t great exercise—it is a wonderful way to pass time. I sometimes listen to audiobooks when I play, and that’s nice, too. ☺️
Not working when I load the game up. Just getting weird patterns . What’s going on?!?

I won’t complain about the price cause I play real tables and it’s not a cheap hobby…

Anyway, this pinball game is very disappointing, first you can’t use your hands to nudge the ball you need to use the analog stick. Which defeats the purpose of this game IMO.

About physics, any serious player knows the tilt bulb has a preferred direction so you can push the table with little to no consequences but you need to be careful going left to right. Nothing like this here, you push 3 times any direction and it’s over.

I could go on and on about the way the ball tends to « brake » when it reaches the top of the bat, which is not what happens in real life, the spin tends to make it « climb ».

Too many problems to make it a cheap and quiet alternative to a real table and make your wife happy to not see your collection in the living room.

I recommend you buy a PSP or a PS3 and get « Williams pinball classics » which does everything better than this pitiful cash grab.
Won't let me play because I don't have a gamepad

Where are the new tables in VR, on sale across other 2D platforms and new tables but nothing for the VR people that supported you developers and bought the game. Great game but no further  support from ZEN. Shame.

reviews.review_title: Don’t buy the classsic extension pack
reviews.review_description:
The game is great, but I just bought the classic extension pack (Et, Jaws and Back to the Future) and I’m very disappointed. 

Played a few minutes with it and it is ABSOLUTELY not worth the 30$ it cost. The problem is that we can’t get a refund with a DLC… Wish someone had told me not to bother with that extension pack…

UPDATE: So sad that the devs have given up on this one. But what's so interesting & funny is that the devs (Zen Studios) are the ones dropping another pinball game called Pinball FX VR (3 April)! What!?! Lol. Just from checking out the trailer, it looks like maybe y'all got that one right 😂. We'll see...

ORIGINAL REVIEW: I've noticed the devs don't respond to anyone(maybe I just haven't seen it yet). Hopefully u guys will start doing so 😊. Anyways, I just started playing. Pretty cool so far. My only problem/question is, why is there only 3 spots for the machines? With all that space in that room(i.e. between the back doors, alongside the dining table etc), why don't u have them spread out within the room? U could easily put 4 more machines in that room or that upstairs loft area. Please consider allowing more areas for other machines without having to swap them out between 3 spots. Thanks
Was enjoying game play experience, but assumed when purchased there would be a progression element to unlock more tables. Three just isn't enough for price paid.

playing Pinball is all that honestly, really enjoy it but the fact that i purchased 2 ADD ONs for total of $28-30$ bucks assuming it was going to be all of the tables remaining since it doesn’t explain anything with purchasing the ADD ON & i was only giving 4 extra tables is a ripoff honestly!! 22$ for jaws, ET & back to future..& 6$ bucks for walking dead..just a heads up for everyone thinking about buying the ADD Ons, you’ll still be without 4-5 tables & 50$ bucks in already!! ZEN should mail every customer a box of condoms in return for RAPING us!!

developer_response.body: Hi LuKeMoE98! Thank you for your honest feedback! I will forward this to the developers, and I hope we can make some adjustments. I can guarantee you that our is not to rip off anyone. We would like to apologize if you feel like being ripped of. Have a nice day and thank you for playing!

reviews.review_description: Y'all advertised ALL the tables!!🤬 Only reason I don't ask for a refund is the enjoyment of the 3 tables, but y'all still shouldn't cheat people like that🤬

developer_response.body: Hi Krys1978! Thank you for writing and for your feedback. Sorry about the inconveniences, I will forward your feedback to the relevant people/team to have a look on it. Thank you for playing with our games. Have a great day!

reviews.review_description:
Great game,however, the flippers stick in the up position often. Sticky flippers in all the pinball I have played. 
This greatly takes away from the games and I can only play for a short time as this is very annoying.

Current Status:

The DLC is back now!  Safe to buy again. 🙂

Update:

A few days ago they released an patch that finally sorted out the glitch on the Paranormal table.

I've updated my rating to a very solid five stars.

Update:

It's been over a year since I reported the issue with the graphics on the Paranormal machine to the developers and the issue is still there. The table looks awful, and as I result I don't play it. The rest of the machines are excellent however and I still highly recommend this to any fan of pinball, but just be aware that the Paranormal machine doesn't match the visual quality of the rest of the game and it seems likely that it never will.

On the positive side, the exit door glitch is gone, and it has taken the #1 spot away from the PSVR version.

Original Review:

This is my third time buying this game, (I also have it on PSVR and the Go), so the pricing structure wasn't much of a surprise. Within 30 minutes I'd bought all the DLC, again. :D

Without question, Pinball FX2 VR is my favorite video game pinball, and as close as I'm likely to ever get to owning real pinball machines. The Quest version looks great, closer to the visual quality of the PSVR version than the Go version, (with one exception noted below). The added ability to freely walk around the machines adds a lot to the immersion. It may end up knocking the PSVR version down to second place because of the wireless roomscale.

There are a few issues however that keep me from giving it five stars. There's an occasional stutter if you're walking around. The PSVR version had this when leaning down to study the tables, and they patched it. (Unfortunately the tables released for PSVR in the Universal pack have the stutter issue, and they haven't been patched.)

But my major complaint, (and one I really hope the developers fix since it's one of my favorite machines), is the texture quality on the Paranormal machine. The playfield art is incredibly blurry, even at a distance. All the other machines look sharp and beautifully detailed, even when you get close.

And finally, another review mentioned a serious Exit Door glitch. I read that before I played the game so I just exited with the menu, but even then it didn't reload my library listing until I clicked navigate, then back to library, which makes me a little nervous.

I hope Zen patches the issues, because overall it's a fantastic game and I want to be able to give it a five star review.

Feels like a fit of false advertising when you buy this game. Base game is $15 but your only allowed access to 3 machines. Then it's another $55 for the other pinball games. Lacking content for what it is.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thanks for the honest feedback, and sorry we did not hit the mark! I will forward this to the developers, and I hope we can do better in the future. Thank you for trying the game, and sorry again for not meeting your expectations! Have a nice day!

reviews.review_description: The first tables I purchased are fantastic so thought I would give the universal classic a go. It's terrible! The balls on all 3 tables are jittery and the table colours are too light so the ball is hard to see. I asked for a refund on just the classic but because it is an add-on couldn't get refund. So disappointed as I was expecting them to be as good as first three. Waisted my money and won't be buying the other tables as felt ripped off
Good for a few games but when I didn't opt to buy more  machines all my purchased machines disappeared so I couldn't play anything.  Followed all Oculus suggestions but nothing worked.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thanks for your feedback! Could I ask if you purchased the base game which contains three tables but you can't access them? If the problem is still there, don't hesitate to contact us at support@zenstudios.com.

reviews.review_description:
I bought all tables for £45 excluding The Walking Dead.  The game is OK but I could not recommend it to anyone who isn't a pinball fanatic.  I give this 3 stars .  The price is the highest it could feasibly be and what you get is the absolute bare minimum for your money unlike so many Quest games that go that extra mile.

Pros:
Good ball physics and responsive controls.
Some tables are well designed like Jaws.

Cons:
No VR hands in-game making the port seem a little rushed and lazy.  Same with the look-at-table-to-select main menu.

Annoying 3 table max in room.

No option to toggle music off

No positional controls (sitting I always appear too high above the table so have to wrestle with the Oculus repositioning system... Would have been so easy to include a control to move the table, but no.)

Some very bad design choices like the Biolab table jerking the whole machine when you hit a particular feature which is jarring and obnoxious in VR and feels like a loss of tracking.  Makes that table unplayable for me.

Voice acting on Back To The Future is dubious and lack of shadows under the car model make it look cheap and nasty.

Complete lack of any reflections or even pseudo reflections on the pinball itself suck too: if real reflections are prohibitive because of the Quest's lack of power they could have at least tried some pseudo/fake reflections instead of a shaded gray ball.

Project is seemingly never updated.  I like to support developers who endeavour to make their games the best they can be.  I don't think Zen Studios is that kind of developer.

Okay, let’s get this out of the way. The tables are good, and the physics are great. If you want to walk around with some portable pinball machines, this game will be right up your alley. But there are some issues.

First of all, the value proposition is just ridiculous. You only get three tables with the base game, and the DLC packs cost more than the base game, and since you’re only getting three out of a total of 12 tables it kind of feels like you just paid $14.99 for a quarter of the available content. It feels like the base game should’ve been released as freemium content, then it would’ve been okay to include only three out of twelve tables and ask us to pay more to try out the rest. As it now stands, if you want a complete set, you’ll have to pay another FIFTY ONE dollars on top of the $15 you already paid, making this (if you want the ‘complete’ game) - a $66 dollar purchase. I think that’s kind of insane.

Now for the gameplay, like I said, the physics are good, but the game hardly takes advantage of motion controls. Why do I push a button to launch balls rather than pull the launcher? Why do I push a button to nudge the table rather than just grab and nudge the table? Details like this make it seem like a thoughtless port, and the cost of the DLC’s make it seem like a cash grab.

Only played the Mars table a few times, and I'm already blown away! Fantastic! Been playing (real) pinball since the 1970s and now I feel like I have my own machine. Best thing since they discovered the hatch in Lost. Nuff said.

Such a shame, very good game but as other people have touched on, the whole score keeping for personal leader boards and inputting your initials is not present so can't keep track within the family. Almost like the creators have tossed it aside and not kept up the maintenance.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you for the honest feedback! I will forward this to the relevant people. I hope we can make some adjustments! Thank you for playing and sorry for the inconvenience!

reviews.review_description: Nice graphics fells very realistic and a friends leader board which makes it fun BUT only a a very few tables which let's it down there's loads more tables to buy but are quite expensive, hopefully I'll be able to grab some more tables if there come down in price.

So neat for the quest that you can look all around the pinball machines and the table is right at waist height like a real machine.  Really feels like I am playing pinball!  When compared to $400 for that new mini video pinball table or thousands for a real table this money is easily well spent!

reviews.review_title: Best way to take a poop!
reviews.review_description:
You know sometimes you gotta go into the office for a little sit down time and bad in the old days you'd probably have the same old hunting and fishing or spank mags sitting on the top of the porcelain cover, or in a basket if you were fancy.

Then came mobile phones and you'd be messaging your friends, significant other or even your mom while doing your dirty work. Or you'd spend time posting the food you ate at a restaurant earlier, that you're currently dumping out on fb or IG, or even playing some wonky phone game like Candy Crush or Angry Birds.

Well now there's a WAY better way. I honestly think that pinball and toilets should be sold together or at least have them as accessories, where you can add on a pinball machine. Well now you can.

There's nothing better and before you know it you just hit the jackpot in more ways than one.

In all seriousness though. This is the PERFECT idea for VR. Pinball. I've been even considering getting a machine or 2 in my loft but this is even better.

DEVELOPERS:.

My suggestions.

You NEED

Black Hole
Black Knight

Maybe Hercules 

Would be cool to take those classics and modernize them with quests and led boards in the back with missions and quests added to the originals.

That's all for now.. Duty calls.
Amazing realistic but why no Vr hands ? Game play should be better

I really like this pinball vr experience.  It would be cooler if all the tables could be displayed instead of only 3 at a time.  Kind of like walking into an arcade on the boardwalk.  

Also, I would love, love, love for you to create an add-on for the classic Williams tables, like the 10 that are an old Wii game (Gorgar, Pinbot, Funhouse, Black Knight, Space Shuttle, Whirlwind, Firepower, Taxi, Jive Time & Sorcerer).  

Thank you.

Real nice graphics....but would love it even more if they ad the marvel ones.....:-).
Anyway in my opinion......nice buy....
I there a way to view what game come in what pack?  I am a big fan of playing this !
This is the most fun ever. I love the western one. The movie pack is awesome. I wish there would be holiday themes for Christmas and Halloween.
Often the left and right flippers are « frozen» and don’t want to move until 0,5 s approximately. It only happens with each controller’s trigger and not with the  grip buttons. I’m a quest 2 user.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you for the feedback! Have you experienced this issue in other games as well? If you still see this issue, please write to us at support@zenstudios.com

reviews.review_title: What table would you like to see next
reviews.review_description: Mine would be fun house
This game is great for our family!

As a pinball player for too many years to mention and having my own pinball table , I was impressed on how realistic the game is and have thoroughly enjoyed playing it. And looking forward to buying the add-ons. I have recommended it to several friends and family.If you are thinking of buying it I suggest you do.

Advanced state as yet but:
Animations besite tablea should be far more extended !!!
Audio should be available in German
All the tables are very good..... you can play for hours!
Watched a utube on this game and said 9 tables on basic package...but only three when purchased!

reviews.review_title: Pinball itself plays great - UI issues
reviews.review_description:
Would give it 5 stars except 2 things:

1) The UI in the menus is a mess. A bunch of text appears on top of other text. I don’t know how this made it through 5min of QA testing. 

2) Lack of tables. While fun, I don’t need a whiz-bang experience that’s not feasible in real life (I.e. things floating up out of the tables). Would love to see real world tables available for purchase like theater of magic, twilight zone, Addams family, etc. I’d think they could just be dropped in assuming the engine behind the game is the same as regular pinballFX - they don’t need any fancy 3d added to them to be fun. 


If those 2 were addressed, this would easily be 5 stars. Would save me from likely buying an Arcade1Up machine.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you for the honest review! I Will forward your second point to the relevant people! The newest patch introduced a solution to 1), and I hope we can make more adjustments in the future!

reviews.review_description: l had pinball machines and this vr got it right. l would love to see some recreations of some old favorites as vr gives all the fun with out the need for space and maintance. The 3 games are very good for hours of fun and allows us to play something diffrent to all the other games out there.

reviews.review_title: Awesome  having a near real Pinball exp
reviews.review_description:
It's good. It's real good. 3btables you give u are pretty fun. Especially the Mars and Sea tables. Fx are great. Play is fast and not floaty.
Makes virtual pin seem old and lame. VR pin is a much more realistic experience. 
Graphics a superb ...and being able to walk around machine and see it all is very cool.
The fx they have around while u are playing is neat to watch in-between balls. 
I did adjust my play veiw to project over a real pinball machine to lean on and it was nice to have that real world object to get u in it more. I'm sure a table..chair or a homemade box would work as well if u wanted. So far the is on the top of my list in VR experiences so far. Totally worth the money. Almost makes me want to buy all the other tables but I'll wait till a sale I guess since they are a bit pricey. And I only really  wantt  one ortwo of the tables in dlc pack badly.
If you a pinball fan or not this is a great addition to your collection. I cant say it enough on how much fun and great these are...just buy it and let the good times roll. I'm sure I won't regret it.
